column1: # SNIPPETS TAB — paste into the 'Snippets' sheet
# Each chapter needs 6-8 flashcard snippets (term + definition).

column4: definition
An arrangement that follows a fixed rule and can be extended predictably.
An ordered list of numbers or objects following a rule.
Numbers that can form a triangular dot arrangement.
The product of a number multiplied by itself.
A sequence where each term is the sum of the two previous terms starting from 1, 1.
Numbers divisible by 2.
Numbers not divisible by 2.
A triangular arrangement where each entry is the sum of the two entries above it.
An exact location in space with no size, no length, no width.
column6: Tip of a pencil represents a point

column4: Part of a line with two definite endpoints and a measurable length.
column6: The edge of a ruler is a line segment

column4: Part of a line that starts at one endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction.
column6: A beam of light from a torch

column4: An angle whose measure is greater than 0° and less than 90°.
An angle whose measure is greater than 90° and less than 180°.
Two angles whose measures add up to exactly 90°.
Two angles whose measures add up to exactly 180°.
Angles formed opposite each other when two lines intersect — always equal.
column6: When scissors blades cross, opposite angles are equal

column4: The value of a digit based on its position in the number.
The actual value of a digit, regardless of its position.
column6: In 5476, face value of 4 is always 4

column4: A number that reads the same forwards and backwards.
The number 6174, reached by any 4-digit number (with at least 2 distinct digits) after repeated Kaprekar operations.
